functions.name: Uses prototype pollution protection
functions.description: This code uses Object.freeze, cloneDeep, or Object.create(null) to prevent prototype pollution - good security practice.

recommendation.title: Good: Prototype pollution protection in use
recommendation.description: This code properly protects against prototype pollution vulnerabilities.

action.message: Uses prototype pollution protection (good security practice)

functions.name: Hardcoded API keys/secrets
functions.description: API keys and secrets should be stored in environment variables, not in source code.

recommendation.title: Remove hardcoded secrets
recommendation.description: API keys and secrets should be stored in environment variables, not in source code.

catches: Hardcoded API keys, secrets, tokens, or passwords in source files
AWS keys, Stripe keys, database credentials embedded in code
Private keys stored directly in variables
fix: Move all secrets to environment variables using process.env
Use a secrets manager like AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault
Never commit .env files to version control

action.message: Check for findHardcodedSecret

functions.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_ on secret variables
functions.description: Variables with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix are exposed to the browser. Use them only for public config.

recommendation.title: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix from secret variables
recommendation.description: Variables with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix are exposed to the browser. Use them only for public config.

catches: NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix on secret environment variables
Private keys, API secrets, or tokens exposed to client-side code
fix: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix from any secret variables
Use server-side only environment variables for sensitive data

action.message: Check for findExposedNextjsSecrets

params.message: Secret variable exposed to client with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix

functions.name: Logging sensitive data
functions.description: Logging sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII). Use redaction or exclude from logs.

recommendation.title: Remove sensitive data from logs
recommendation.description: Logging sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII). Use redaction or exclude from logs.

catches: Logging passwords, tokens, API keys, or other secrets
Logging PII like emails, phone numbers, or addresses
Logging full request bodies that may contain sensitive data
fix: Use structured logging that excludes sensitive fields
Never log req.body, headers, or authentication tokens

action.message: Check for findSensitiveLogging

params.message: Sensitive data being logged - redact passwords, tokens, PII

functions.name: Credentials in URL parameters
functions.description: Credentials in URL params are visible in browser history and logs. Use headers or body instead.

recommendation.title: Remove credentials from URL
recommendation.description: Credentials in URL params are visible in browser history and logs. Use headers or body instead.

catches: API keys or passwords passed in URL query parameters
Credentials visible in browser history and server logs
Tokens or secrets in URL paths
fix: Move credentials to HTTP headers or request body
Use Authorization header with Bearer tokens

action.message: Check for findUrlCredentials

params.message: Credentials in URL - use headers or body instead

functions.name: Cookie without secure flag
functions.description: Cookies with credentials should have Secure and HttpOnly flags set.

recommendation.title: Add secure flag to cookies
recommendation.description: Cookies with credentials should have Secure and HttpOnly flags set.

catches: Cookies set without Secure flag (can be sent over HTTP)
Cookies without HttpOnly flag (accessible via JavaScript)
fix: Add HttpOnly flag to prevent XSS access
Add SameSite attribute for CSRF protection

action.message: Check for findInsecureCookie

params.message: Cookie without Secure and HttpOnly flags - add cookie options

functions.name: Self-signed certificate usage
functions.description: Self-signed certificates should not be used in production. Use valid SSL certificates.

recommendation.description: Self-signed certificates should not be used in production. Use valid SSL certificates.

functions.name: SQL injection via string concatenation
functions.description: SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries or an ORM.

recommendation.title: Use parameterized queries
recommendation.description: SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries or an ORM.

catches: SQL queries built with string concatenation
Template literals with variables in SQL
User input directly in query strings
fix: Use an ORM like Prisma or Drizzle
Never concatenate user input into SQL strings

action.message: Potential SQL injection - string concatenation in query

functions.name: Command injection via exec/eval
functions.description: Command injection risk. Avoid shell execution with unsanitized input.

recommendation.title: Avoid exec/eval with user input
recommendation.description: Command injection risk. Avoid shell execution with unsanitized input.

catches: Using exec() or execSync() with string concatenation
Dynamic code execution with user-controlled strings

Use child_process with array arguments instead of shell strings
Use JSON.parse instead of eval for data

functions.name: IDOR - missing ownership verification
